2026-04-17T22:44:37.6345970Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6346543Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6346988Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6347350Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6347667Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6347911Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6348078Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6348282Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6348463Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6348712Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T22:44:37.6349237Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T22:44:37.6349503Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T22:45:58.9659260Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T22:45:43.680281031Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58320,\"title\":\"Remove `04_core_feature_request` and `05_mqissue` issue templates\",\"body\":\"### Summary\\n\\nRemove two internal-use issue templates that are no longer serving their intended purpose and are contributing to noise:\\n\\n- **`04_core_feature_request.yml`** — \\u0026#34;Core Feature Request\\u0026#34; template intended for Azure SDK authors to request `Azure.Core` infrastructure features\\n- **`05_mqissue.yml`** — \\u0026#34;MQ Improvements Tracker\\u0026#34; template for tracking milestone-of-quality work items\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\n**`05_mqissue`** has a minimal form (single textarea) with no guidance, making it indistinguishable from a blank issue. It attracts significant spam — many issues filed through it are immediately locked and closed as `not_planned`. The MQ initiative can use blank issues with manual labeling or a dedicated project board instead.\\n\\n**`04_core_feature_request`** targets a narrow internal audience (\\u0026#34;Azure SDK authors\\u0026#34;) but is visible to all public users in the issue chooser. Its structured fields (API change needed, API proposal, time constraints, stakeholders) are valuable for internal planning but would be better served by a team-internal process. SDK authors can use the general feature request template (02) or a blank issue with the `Azure.Core` label.\\n\\n### Proposed changes\\n\\n1. **Delete** `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/04_core_feature_request.yml`\\n2. **Delete** `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/05_mqissue.yml`\\n3. **Rename** `06_learn_feedback.yml` → `04_learn_feedback.yml` to maintain sequential numbering with no gaps\\n4. **No changes needed** to `config.yml` (`blank_issues_enabled: true` already allows freeform issues)\\n5. **No workflow changes needed** — no GitHub Actions reference either template filename\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\n| Item | Impact |\\n|------|--------|\\n| Auto-assignment (`christothes`, `annelo-msft`, `JoshLove-msft`, `jsquire` on Core; `pallavit` on MQ) | Lost — use manual assignment or a labeler action |\\n| Auto-labeling (`Azure.Core`, `client`, `feature-request`, `needs-team-triage` on Core; `MQ`, `Client` on MQ) | Lost — use manual labeling or blank issues |\\n| `MQ` and `Azure.Core` labels | **No change** — labels are independent of templates and remain on existing issues |\\n| Existing issues filed via these templates | **No change** — already created issues are unaffected |\\n| learn.microsoft.com feedback | **Rename only** — `06` → `04`, no functional change (GitHub resolves templates by `name`, not filename) |\\n\\n### Alternatives considered\\n\\n- **Hide instead of delete**: GitHub issue template forms don\\u0026#39;t support a `hidden` property. The only way to remove them from the chooser is deletion.\\n- **Restrict via description warning**: `04` already has a warning (\\u0026#34;this template is intended to be used by Azure SDK authors\\u0026#34;), but it doesn\\u0026#39;t prevent public use.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58320\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T22:43:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T22:43:13Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-17T22:45:58.9671701Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T22:45:43.692188525Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58320,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T22:45:58.9685508Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T22:45:43.875960765Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58320,\"title\":\"Remove `04_core_feature_request` and `05_mqissue` issue templates\",\"body\":\"### Summary\\n\\nRemove two internal-use issue templates that are no longer serving their intended purpose and are contributing to noise:\\n\\n- **`04_core_feature_request.yml`** — \\u0026#34;Core Feature Request\\u0026#34; template intended for Azure SDK authors to request `Azure.Core` infrastructure features\\n- **`05_mqissue.yml`** — \\u0026#34;MQ Improvements Tracker\\u0026#34; template for tracking milestone-of-quality work items\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\n**`05_mqissue`** has a minimal form (single textarea) with no guidance, making it indistinguishable from a blank issue. It attracts significant spam — many issues filed through it are immediately locked and closed as `not_planned`. The MQ initiative can use blank issues with manual labeling or a dedicated project board instead.\\n\\n**`04_core_feature_request`** targets a narrow internal audience (\\u0026#34;Azure SDK authors\\u0026#34;) but is visible to all public users in the issue chooser. Its structured fields (API change needed, API proposal, time constraints, stakeholders) are valuable for internal planning but would be better served by a team-internal process. SDK authors can use the general feature request template (02) or a blank issue with the `Azure.Core` label.\\n\\n### Proposed changes\\n\\n1. **Delete** `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/04_core_feature_request.yml`\\n2. **Delete** `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/05_mqissue.yml`\\n3. **Rename** `06_learn_feedback.yml` → `04_learn_feedback.yml` to maintain sequential numbering with no gaps\\n4. **No changes needed** to `config.yml` (`blank_issues_enabled: true` already allows freeform issues)\\n5. **No workflow changes needed** — no GitHub Actions reference either template filename\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\n| Item | Impact |\\n|------|--------|\\n| Auto-assignment (`christothes`, `annelo-msft`, `JoshLove-msft`, `jsquire` on Core; `pallavit` on MQ) | Lost — use manual assignment or a labeler action |\\n| Auto-labeling (`Azure.Core`, `client`, `feature-request`, `needs-team-triage` on Core; `MQ`, `Client` on MQ) | Lost — use manual labeling or blank issues |\\n| `MQ` and `Azure.Core` labels | **No change** — labels are independent of templates and remain on existing issues |\\n| Existing issues filed via these templates | **No change** — already created issues are unaffected |\\n| learn.microsoft.com feedback | **Rename only** — `06` → `04`, no functional change (GitHub resolves templates by `name`, not filename) |\\n\\n### Alternatives considered\\n\\n- **Hide instead of delete**: GitHub issue template forms don\\u0026#39;t support a `hidden` property. The only way to remove them from the chooser is deletion.\\n- **Restrict via description warning**: `04` already has a warning (\\u0026#34;this template is intended to be used by Azure SDK authors\\u0026#34;), but it doesn\\u0026#39;t prevent public use.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58320\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T22:43:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T22:43:13Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
